member.name: Graham Stuart
member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Graham Stuart is the Conservative MP for Beverley and Holderness , and has been an MP continually since 5 May 2005.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, if he will publish the full list of energy projects whose funding has been delayed, reduced or cancelled to meet the capital reductions required to finance the Defence Investment Plan, togeth
writtenQuestions.answerText: The savings are a small part of DESNZ’s overall budget, which saw a significant uplift at Spending Review. Our aim is for a large proportion of the savings to be found through efficiencies. We will set out more details in due course. The UK clean energy e...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Local Transport Fund: East Riding
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what progress he has made on releasing funding for East Riding of Yorkshire Council from the Local Transport Fund.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Transport pays local transport funding to Local Transport Authorities (LTAs). East Riding of Yorkshire council is a constituent authority of Hull and East Yorkshire Combined Authority, which acts as the LTA. Hull and East Yorkshire Combi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Iron and Steel: Import Duties
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what estimate her Department has made of the potential annual revenue to be generated by tariffs under the new steel trade measure; and whether any of this revenue will be used to mitigate cost increas
writtenQuestions.answerText: The purpose of the trade measure is not to raise tariff revenue, and therefore we have not made any estimates. The Government is acting in response to the serious threat posed by global steel overcapacity. This continues to distort markets, and threaten t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Iron and Steel: Imports
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what assessment her Department has made of the potential impact of the 1 July 2026 implementation date for the steel trade measure on businesses with procurement lead times in excess of three months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The new trade measure is being applied in response to significant threat of global steel overcapacity. The steel safeguard expired on 30 June under WTO rules. The UK cannot risk a gap in protection, which would see the loss of steelmaking in the UK and le...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Iron and Steel: Manufacturing Industries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, what steps he is taking to help reduce levels of industrial electricity costs for UK steel producers compared to EU ones.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are reducing industrial energy prices for steel businesses through the measures set out in the Industrial and Steel Strategies: the EII compensation Scheme, the uplift to the British Industry Supercharger, and the British Industrial Competitiveness Sch...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Business and Trade

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what assessment he has made of the Armed Forces’ assessment that an additional £28 billion in funding is required over the next four years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Spending levels on Defence are a matter for the Prime Minister and Chancellor, and we will publish the Defence Investment Plan before the NATO summit. The Defence Investment Plan will be both affordable and deliverable and will deliver the best kit into t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, when his Department plans to move projects shortlisted under the second Hydrogen Allocation Round to the Invite to Offer stage of negotiations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are working hard across government to start the Invite to Offer stage of HAR2 as soon as possible and will be in touch with projects when this commences. We understand that many projects are at critical stages, and that certainty is important to suppor...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to ensure the timely delivery of the Hydrogen Allocation Round 2 scheme by end of 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are working hard across government to start the Invite to Offer stage of HAR2 as soon as possible and will be in touch with projects when this commences. We understand that many projects are at critical stages, and that certainty is important to suppor...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen: Supply Chains
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of delays to the renewed Hydrogen Strategy on the UK hydrogen technology supply chain.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We understand the importance of providing clarity to businesses across the hydrogen sector, including those in the supply chain. Hydrogen is backed in the Industrial Strategy Clean Energy Industries Sector Plan as a frontier technology, with a public fina...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Mobile Phones: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether her Department has assessed the most popular smartphone applications used by 8 to 14-year-olds after (a) 21:00 and (b) 23:00.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Protecting children online is a priority for the Secretary of State and this government. That is why we published the ‘Growing up in the Online World’ consultation, accompanied by a National Conversation on 2 March 2026.The consultation asked for views on...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Internet: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether her Department has made an assessment of the online services where eight to 14-year-olds are most likely to encounter harmful content.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Children’s safety and wellbeing online is the Secretary of State’s top priority. That is why we published the ‘Growing up in the Online World’ consultation, accompanied by a National Conversation on 2 March 2026.The government published a progress report ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: HM Coastguard: Voluntary Work
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the financial impact on Coastguard Rescue Officers who lose income from their primary employment when responding to emergency callouts; and whether she plans to introd
writtenQuestions.answerText: The revised operating model for the Coastguard Rescue Service (CRS) has been agreed following a Court of Appeal ruling, which meant that the current remuneration model could not legally continue.We deeply value and recognise the significant service Coastg...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, when he plans to update the UK Hydrogen Strategy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We plan to publish a renewed Hydrogen Strategy, alongside a package of other hydrogen policy documents, as soon as possible.The renewed Hydrogen Strategy will set out Government’s vision and objectives for hydrogen, and how we intend to work together with...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Special Educational Needs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, with reference to her Department's White Paper entitled Every child achieving and thriving, updated on 27 April 2026, if she will set out how the proposed targeted layer of support for children with SEND will d
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department is investing £1.6 billion in an Inclusive Mainstream Fund to support the development of a more inclusive education system. Over £500 million per year will be provided to early years, schools and colleges to boost their existing core funding...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Functional Neurological Disorder
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether psychological therapy services are fully commissioned across all areas of England to accept referrals for patients with Functional Neurological Disorder and non-epileptic seizures; and what
writtenQuestions.answerText: Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for commissioning services that meet the needs of their local populations and for ensuring that providers deliver services in line with national service specifications. NHS England oversees this process and ha...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Functional Neurological Disorder
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what guidance exists on which clinician - GP or neurologist - is responsible for reviewing and managing medication for patients with Functional Neurological Disorder and non-epileptic seizures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for commissioning services that meet the needs of their local populations and for ensuring that providers deliver services in line with national service specifications. NHS England oversees this process and ha...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Functional Neurological Disorder
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps he will take where NHS trusts tell patients with Functional Neurological Disorder that their services are not set up to treat the condition, in the context of NHS England's August 2025 S
writtenQuestions.answerText: Integrated care boards (ICBs) are responsible for commissioning services that meet the needs of their local populations and for ensuring that providers deliver services in line with national service specifications. NHS England oversees this process and ha...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Radioactive Waste: Storage
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, that the consent based model for determining the location of GDFs for storing nuclear waste remains government policy.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government remains committed to delivering a Geological Disposal Facility (GDF) as the long-term solution for the safe and secure management of the UK’s most hazardous radioactive waste. The process for identifying a suitable location for a GDF remain...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Motorcycles: Safety
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she plans to introduce a strategy for motorcycle safety.
writtenQuestions.answerText: By miles travelled, motorcyclists are over 40 times more likely to be killed or seriously injured than someone travelling by car. That is why motorcyclist safety was included in the new Road Safety Strategy. Although there are no plans for a separate moto...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Hydrogen: Humber Estuary
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of the potential impact of the Humber’s hydrogen production capacity on UK hydrogen targets.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Hydrogen has the potential to support decarbonisation and economic growth across the UK. The Government recognises the Humber as one of several regions that could supply, as well as benefit from, low‑carbon hydrogen production.As part of the Hydrogen Allo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero
